title: Top search “See all results” click passes a non-event into submit handler
reasoning: handleSearchSubmit assumes it receives a form submit event and unconditionally calls preventDefault. The dropdown's “See all results” control wires the same function to a button onClick, where React passes a mouse event. That still has preventDefault, so the current top-search button works by accident. The handler's API is misleading and fragile: any programmatic call or reuse from a non-DOM event path will throw before navigation. This is concrete because the same function is already being used for two different event contracts in this file.
reproduction: Call handleSearchSubmit without a DOM event, or refactor the dropdown to call it from a keyboard/action helper, and it will throw TypeError before navigating.
recommendation: Separate the search action from event handling: create a submitSearch() function that reads searchQuery and navigates, and have form onSubmit call e.preventDefault() before submitSearch(); have button onClick call submitSearch directly.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No tests were included for DocsLayout search behavior or for the dropdown “See all results” action.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add an interaction test that opens top search results, clicks “See all results,” and asserts navigation to /docs/search?q=... without relying on form submit semantics.
minimumFixScope: Update DocsLayout search handlers around handleSearchSubmit and the dropdown button.
